f64_fixture_version: pending — F64 reference logits not yet generated for the 3B variant; tests/fixtures/llama32_3b_reference/expected_logits_f64.json missing. The 1B sibling has a fixture; 3B is structurally identical (same RoPE llama3 scaling, same builder) and a future PyTorch F64 generation pass can backfill it.
generated_on: RTX 4070 Laptop (sm_89), 8 GB VRAM, 32 GB RAM, Windows 11
config_compatibility_notes: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct shares the same architectural template as Llama 3.2 1B (M4.6 Phase C) — RoPE llama3 piecewise frequency scaling, GQA, tied word embeddings — scaled to 28 layers / hidden=3072 / intermediate=8192 / head_dim=128 explicit. The eos_token_id array variant landed in M11.A.1 (commit ac70581) and applies here.

certified_mode.validation_notes: F64 fixture not yet generated for Llama 3.2 3B (~5.98 GiB BF16 fits the dev box at F64 with ~12 GiB peak — feasible, just queued for a separate PyTorch reference run). Validation today via greedy smoke under both modes.

fast_mode.validation_notes: Drift envelope inherited from ADR-005 family characterisation. Llama 3.2 1B (the F64-fixture sibling) under fast mode shows drift 0.268 with ADR-004 strict 1.9× margin and a position-2 argmax flip; the 3B variant likely tracks similarly but a calibrated number requires the queued F64 fixture generation.

smoke_validation.prompt: Tell me about the history of Rome
smoke_validation.certified_text: The history of Rome is a long and complex one, spanning over 2,500 years. Here
smoke_validation.fast_text: The history of Rome is a long and complex one, spanning over 2,500 years. Here
smoke_validation.interpretation: Bit-identical generated text under both modes. Llama 3.2 3B fits comfortably in dev-box memory under both modes; fast mode tier-plan moves nearly the entire model into VRAM (196 / 254 = 77 % of GPU-eligible weights) thanks to BF16 storage doubling capacity. Speedup 1.57× — the largest fast/certified ratio in the M11.A family because the model is smaller and a higher fraction of matmuls hit the fast kernel.

recommended_mode_rationale: Same conservative position as Llama 3.2 1B: certified passes ADR-004 strict on the 1B sibling with 51× margin (drift 0.0097); the 3B is structurally identical and is expected to behave similarly when the F64 fixture is generated. Fast mode produced bit-identical argmax in this single-prompt smoke; the 1B sibling under fast mode flipped argmax at position 2, so operators wanting the 1.57× lift via env override should validate against their actual prompts.
per_tensor_policy_status: Schema v2.0.0 (M11.A.2) — same family-wide structural heuristic as the rest of the certified set: FFN gate/up/down on fast, attention Q/K/V/O on certified. Mirrors the Llama 3.2 1B sibling's manifest exactly. Empirical refinement deferred until the F64 fixture for 3B is generated.

verification.command_certified: ATENIA_M8_BF16_KERNEL=1 atenia generate --prompt 'Tell me about the history of Rome' --model models/llama-3.2-3b-instruct --max-tokens 20
verification.command_fast: ATENIA_FAST_MODE=1 atenia generate --prompt 'Tell me about the history of Rome' --model models/llama-3.2-3b-instruct --max-tokens 20
verification.expected_text_prefix: The history of Rome is a long and complex one, spanning over 2,500 years
